name: Plain-text password written to database
fix: Hash the password with bcrypt or argon2 before storing: const hash = await bcrypt.hash(req.body.password, 12).
breach_precedent: RockYou 2009: 32M plain-text passwords leaked. Adobe 2013: 153M weakly-hashed passwords leaked.

name: Weak password hashing (MD5)
fix: MD5 is broken for passwords. Use bcrypt (cost >= 12) or argon2.

name: Weak password hashing (SHA-1)
fix: SHA-1 is broken for passwords. Use bcrypt (cost >= 12) or argon2.

name: Unsalted SHA-256 used for password
fix: Plain SHA-256 is too fast and easy to rainbow-table. Use bcrypt or argon2.

name: bcrypt cost factor too low
fix: Use a bcrypt cost factor of 12 or higher: bcrypt.hash(pw, 12).

name: Open redirect using req.query / req.body
fix: Validate the redirect target against an allowlist of paths or hostnames before calling res.redirect.
breach_precedent: Open redirects are the standard last hop in phishing chains and OAuth account takeovers.

name: Open redirect via window.location = req.query
fix: Never assign window.location from untrusted query strings without an allowlist.

name: Hardcoded JWT signing secret
fix: Never hardcode the JWT secret. Load it from process.env.JWT_SECRET and use a long random value.
breach_precedent: A hardcoded JWT secret lets anyone forge valid tokens and log in as any user.

name: JWT 'none' algorithm allowed
fix: Never allow the 'none' algorithm. Pin a strong one: jwt.verify(token, secret, { algorithms: ['HS256'] }).
breach_precedent: The JWT 'none' algorithm bypass lets attackers submit unsigned tokens that are accepted as valid.

name: TLS certificate validation disabled
fix: Remove rejectUnauthorized: false — it disables TLS checks and enables man-in-the-middle attacks. Use a proper CA certificate.

name: Node TLS verification globally disabled
fix: Remove NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 — it turns off certificate validation for the entire app.

name: Auth cookie missing HttpOnly
fix: Set httpOnly: true on session/auth cookies so JavaScript (and any XSS) can't read them.

name: Insecure randomness for a security token
fix: Math.random() is predictable. Use crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex') or crypto.randomUUID() for tokens, codes, and secrets.
